title: Window Functions, Analytical Grain, and Time-Aware SQL · Cardinality Forensics
summary: Service leaders need rankings, running effort, previous-event comparisons, and rolling metrics without collapsing detail rows. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a time-aware service-level analytics report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Define window partition, order, and frame independently from final result ordering.
Choose ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, and NTILE with explicit tie semantics.
Use LAG, LEAD, FIRST_VALUE, and LAST_VALUE with deliberate ordering and frame behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Time Semantics
steps.instruction:
Order the Time Semantics evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

steps.why: Correct order keeps Time Semantics assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Time Semantics, keep Time Semantics connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Compare each ticket with its team's effort and severity peers, retain teams with no resolved work, and rank ties deliberately. Competency probe: Separate instants, local civil time, duration, calendar boundaries, and incomplete reporting windows.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Time Semantics
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-time-semantics-1tf1g1" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; window partition, ordering, frame, rank, or time evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Time Semantics.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Time Semantics into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Time Semantics, keep Time Semantics connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-time-semantics-1tf1g1
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id, severity,
       DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END) AS severity_rank
FROM tickets ORDER BY severity_rank, ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
